sourceMovie.title: Raya and the Last Dragon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Raya and the Last Dragon (2021) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Don Hall's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kung Fu Panda 4
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Kung Fu Panda 4 (2024) is the kind of film Raya and the Last Dragon fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The animation overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Kung Fu Panda 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Kung Fu Panda 3 shares Raya and the Last Dragon's gift for animation — the 2016 film delivers family without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Wish Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Raya and the Last Dragon compelling — the animation, the family — shows up in Wish Dragon (2021) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Black Cauldron
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The animation energy of Raya and the Last Dragon is alive in The Black Cauldron (1985), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a uplifting payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The War of the Rohirrim
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Lord of the Rings: The War of the Rohirrim (2024) operates in the same animation as Raya and the Last Dragon, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The uplifting payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Monkey King
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Monkey King (2023) belongs to the same conversation as Raya and the Last Dragon — both anchor their animation in family emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: My Little Pony: A New Generation
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Raya and the Last Dragon's animation stayed with you, My Little Pony: A New Generation (2021) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The family through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Finding Nemo
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Raya and the Last Dragon excels in animation, Finding Nemo (2003) doubles down on family to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Ice Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ice Age (2002) is the natural next step after Raya and the Last Dragon: same animation DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and family elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Kung Fu Panda
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Raya and the Last Dragon who respond to its animation will find Kung Fu Panda (2008) immediately familiar. Both films share a family sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

faq.question: What should I watch after Raya and the Last Dragon?
faq.answer: Try Kung Fu Panda 4, Kung Fu Panda 3, Wish Dragon—each shares DNA with Raya and the Last Dragon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Raya and the Last Dragon?
faq.answer: Raya and the Last Dragon (2021) is best described as Animation, Family, Fantasy, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Raya and the Last Dragon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
